name: CASH FOR GOLD & SILVER EXCHANGE - JEWELERS
description_short: Dallas Gold & Silver Exchange buys and sells precious metals, fine jewelry, luxury watches, and bullion across 7 North Texas locations. Established over 40 years ago.
description_long:
Dallas Gold & Silver Exchange (DGSE) is a long-established precious metals and jewelry buyer/seller operating in the North Texas region for over 40 years. The company has built its reputation on authentication, fair valuation, and customer service across jewelry, watches, and bullion transactions. DGSE operates 7 physical locations in Dallas, Frisco, Grand Prairie, Grapevine, Lewisville, Euless, and Fort Worth, positioning itself as a regional alternative to national pawn chains. The business model emphasizes hands-on evaluation and transparency in the buying and selling process.

DGSE's core services include buying and selling fine jewelry (including engagement rings and wedding bands), luxury watches (including Rolex and other timepieces), gold and silver bullion, and precious metals. The company also offers jewelry and watch repair services. Their stated approach is to authenticate items thoroughly before purchase and to provide fair-market valuations. The website emphasizes they do not accept counterfeits or fakes, positioning authentication as a competitive advantage.

The company distinguishes itself through longevity (40+ years in operation), local community focus in North Texas, environmental commitment, and detailed customer testimonials highlighting staff expertise and service quality. Customer reviews specifically mention fair appraisals, knowledgeable staff (including named manager Daniel), proactive customer service (e.g., replacing missing watch bezel without request), and friendly hospitality (offering water/coffee). The business model supports repeat customers, as evidenced by multigenerational family loyalty mentioned in testimonials.

However, DGSE is fundamentally a regional pawn/precious metals dealer, not a consumer finance product. While they provide liquidity by buying jewelry and metals, this is asset liquidation rather than lending. Consumers should understand that selling jewelry to DGSE yields below-retail valuations (as stated: "we don't pay full price so neither should you"). The company's services are geographically limited to North Texas and require in-person visits to physical locations.
pros: 40+ years of operating history and established reputation in North Texas market
Multiple convenient locations (7 stores across Dallas-Fort Worth area) for accessibility
Offers both buying and selling of jewelry, watches, and bullion—providing two-way marketplace liquidity
Provides jewelry and watch repair services alongside buying/selling
Named staff expertise highlighted in customer reviews (authentication, appraisal knowledge, watch repair)
Proactive customer service practices documented (e.g., unsolicited watch bezel replacement)
Explicit authentication process to reject counterfeits and protect buyer confidence
cons: Intentionally pays below retail/fair-market value (company explicitly states they don't pay full price), resulting in significant haircuts on valuations
Limited to North Texas region—7 locations only; not accessible to consumers outside Dallas-Fort Worth metro area
Requires in-person visits to physical stores; no mail-in appraisal or remote selling options mentioned
No information provided on pricing transparency, fee structures, or how appraisals are calculated
Not a lending or credit product—does not address consumer finance needs like emergency cash, personal loans, or credit building
best_for: North Texas residents with jewelry, watches, or bullion to liquidate who prioritize local convenience
Consumers seeking to upgrade engagement rings or jewelry with trade-in options and expert appraisal
Luxury watch buyers/sellers who want hands-on authentication and local expertise without national chain overhead
diagnosis: DGSE is best for North Texas residents with jewelry, watches, or bullion to sell who value local expertise and authentication—not for consumers seeking consumer finance products or credit solutions. The critical caveat is that DGSE pays below retail value by design; consumers should treat this as asset liquidation, not an alternative lending or credit-building product. This company belongs in pawn-shops category, not consumer finance lending.
services: Buy and sell fine jewelry (used and vintage)
Buy and sell engagement rings and wedding bands
Buy and sell luxury watches and timepieces
Buy and sell gold and silver bullion and precious metals
Trade-in options for stones and metals
